The perpetrator was very careful, and seemed to leave no evidence behind... but there were clues in the ashes, and it was up to forensic scientists to find them.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man becomes the prime suspect in his wife's death after forensic science raises questions about her apparent suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Investigators on both sides of the Atlantic seek links between three homicides that look like professional executions.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Who put a bomb in the home of a 29-year-old woman and killed her?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A killer tried to destroy everything which could link them to the crime; but in doing so, they inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light using a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a tycoon's wife is found dead in her SUV submerged in a half-frozen creek, police begin to suspect it wasn't an accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Russian immigrant is found dead and a strange phone call to his office is the only lead investigators have.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Physical evidence initially condemns a prime suspect to a double homicide, but DNA from a discarded spoon points to another.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young girl falls ill and dies; forensic investigators search for evidence halfway around the world to shed light on the tragic death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two bodies are discovered, shot to death in their car; the car windows are broken, but there's no sign of shattered glass.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Key suspect has a solid alibi in the murder of a war veteran; but police get a lead by finding a new suspect with a sweet tooth.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who appeared to have taken her own life told her sister that, if anything were to happen to her, there was a note in the china cabinet.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an aspiring model turns up dead, police suspect her boyfriend. But must dig deeper for clues when he is cleared.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A twelve-year-old girl claims that she was abducted and sexually assaulted; her story seems unbelievable until police discover fibers on her clothing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man calls for help when his wife has a fatal fall down the stairs; numerous amount of bloodstains make police suspicious.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A decade old case reopens, and a few seconds of a 911 call enable investigators to determine who is responsible for a murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A flawed envelope is an unlikely clue to identifying a killer who laced a water cooler with cyanide and caused the death of a woman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mussel diver and his fiancée are found dead in their burnt home; a tiny clue points to arson and murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For ten years, the disappearance of a college co-ed remained a mystery; new scientific testing cast a different light on a man who had been a suspect all along.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Tipster is found dead after revealing that a woman's death 16 years ago was murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of 53-year-old Paul Gruber is examined, and the evidence points to Darryl Robin Kuehl, who was later convicted of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man states that a robber shot and killed his wife while they were on the beach, but a song he downloaded makes investigators suspicious.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Detectives discover new clues left by a stalker's letter to his victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of an insurance assessor is found in a river in Tampa, Fla.; a unique murder weapon and blatant clues lead the police straight to her careless killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a high school athlete is killed in a hit-and-run accident, investigators hoped tiny paint chips and shards of plastic would lead them to the driver.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is abducted and a month later found dead, then a tip leads investigators in the right direction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How the bombs were made leads investigators in Colorado to find the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of an insurance assessor is found in a river in Tampa, Fla.; a unique murder weapon and blatant clues lead the police straight to her careless killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police find fragments of deleted computer files leading them to suspect a woman's suicide is really murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspicious neighbor gives police a break in a cold case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A family suspects their teenage daughter's new friends were involved in her disappearance.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 62-year-old man opens a car restoration business and becomes seriously ill, his doctors cannot find the cause, but forensics can; identification of a highly toxic metal in the victim's blood leads to his poisoner.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 6-year-old disappears, her father becomes the prime suspect; he fails a polygraph test and even confesses, but 10 years later, the investigation gets turned on its head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Investigators build a makeshift tent around a woman's body and use superglue fumes to lift the killer's palm print from the victim's skin.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of a mail-order bride proves suspicious when detectives learn that two of the Texas husband's previous wives had died in mysterious circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A jeweler's search through old sales records helps police find likely suspects in the murder of elderly couple Edward Kowalzck and Gertrude Thompson.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A garbage bag leads police to a child's killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the disappearance of a young woman uncovers a tooth that helps police solve a crime without a body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The sensitive nose of a forensic examiner sniffs out murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Tiny fibers and an asthma inhaler are the clues that lead officers to the killer of an off-duty policeman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Investigators search the world of high rollers and offshore betting to solve the first triple homicide in Verona, Wisc.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police search every apartment building in town when the body of a young girl found on an isolated farm holds a set of keys but no ID.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man denies killing a pedestrian in hit-and-run, claiming he sold the car in question; authorities track down the car to find critical evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three years later, a second autopsy determines who's right when a medical examiner and detectives disagree on the cause of death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hit-and-run boating accident that kills a popular young man causes investigators to search for one boat out of thousands.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead on her bedroom floor, the only clue is a bloodstained sheet; breakthrough forensics enable police to identify the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young man is killed in a mysterious car crash, but the evidence at the scene lead investigators to believe it is not an accident; forensic science reveals what really happens, and the truth devastates three families.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead, police use blood evidence and computer forensics to convince a jury that the murderer is guilty.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an elderly woman dies in an apparent robbery-homicide, evidence suggests the suspect broke out of the house rather than breaking in.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Wealthy man is shot dead while his wife is forced to open their hidden safe, but they couldn't be identified because they were wearing ski masks.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The bomb was constructed to cause as much damage as possible, and it did, killing the victim with deadly force and flame; a painstaking search yielded tiny clues, which identified the killer as surely as if he'd signed them.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: DNA profiling reveals clues leading to a killer's identity 20 years later.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder in Australia baffles police, until they find a device intended to save lives is the only witness to the man's death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man dies after falling down the stairs, a witness says one thing and the evidence says another.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wax bullet, white blanket and olive colored carpeting tell a lot about the murder of a young woman found in a ravine.

programme.title: Weather Gone Viral
programme.desc: People's lives can change in an instant when weather turns violent; excessive rain, wind, snow, violent storms and earthquakes can trigger weather wipeouts.

programme.title: Weather Gone Viral
programme.desc: The nightmare scenarios facing these men and woman as their 9-5 shifts turn into a race to survive; record-breaking snow, torrential rainfall and even tornados - bring a day at the office to a halt.

programme.title: Weather Gone Viral
programme.desc: Ice forces an airplane down and passengers must prepare for a crash landing; a dust devil strikes during a controlled burn, sending flames whipping into the air; a powerful wave drags tourists down a 12-story drop.

programme.title: Weather Gone Viral
programme.desc: Falling deep into a crevasse, swept away from a flash flood, and violently pick-up by a tornado; find out how these people escaped nature's clutches.

programme.title: Weather Gone Viral
programme.desc: From a massive haboob that turns day into night, to a freak hail storm that turns an Arizona pool into a torrent of waves, the most extreme weather, caught on camera.

programme.desc: Tornadoes in outer space; a volcanic eruption that creates a sonic boom; a fog that overwhelms the Grand Canyon and makes it vanish from sight.

programme.desc: Fighting to survive the power of the wind, lethal lightning strikes, and rivers that bring devastating floods in a never-ending battle between man and weather.

programme.desc: Exploring weather phenomena found on planet Earth.

programme.title: Comics Unleashed With Byron Allen
programme.desc: Host and comedian Byron Allen invites four A-List comedians to this round-table comedy talk show. Guest comedians share their own funny perspectives on current events, everyday life, pop culture, entertainment and more. Filmed in front of a live audience in Hollywood, previous guests have included: Dennis Miller, Tom Arnold, Howie Mandel, Jon Lovitz and Cedric the Entertainer.

programme.desc: Pat grills riverside with renowned chef Vivek Surti, crafting lamb mamna and chicken kebabs inspired by his Indian heritage.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: On this special delivery episode of Struggle Meals, Frankie explores three ways to make delicious pizzas, and shares clever tips to make an A+ pie without dropping your dough on expensive ingredients.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Bonsoir, indeed! Join Frankie as he cooks up elegant but achievable French classics that will transport you to an evening on the Seine, complete with some savvy wine tips without the hefty cost of a plane ticket.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Start a new tradition at the dinner table this year with a meat free spin on your favorite main courses. Learn new tips and tricks to make your veggie forward dinner so delightful that even the biggest meat eaters will be back for seconds.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie kicks it up a notch in the Struggle Kitchen with flavor-packed, spicy recipes, and some hot tips for adding layers of flavor. But don't sweat it, these warming dishes won't burn your cash or your tastebuds.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: With the help of a friend, Frankie serves up easy and delicious recipes based on take out favorites everyone will enjoy at home. No need to shell out extra cash for delivery fees!

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Winter citrus is here to brighten up your day. Frankie is fixing up some light, zesty, and affordable recipes to add a little seasonal sunshine to your plate.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: From casual weeknights to special occasions, be the star of the dinnertime with these crowd-pleasing meals.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Put leftovers to good use with tasty, waste-reducing recipes that turn excess food into exciting new dishes.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Give your wallet a break by taking the fine-dining experience straight to the comfort of your home. From tender, juicy steak paired with classic sides, to decadent desserts, Frankie's got your date night meal covered.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Treat yourself to a five star pasta dinner, as Frankie cooks his signature dishes with showstopping sauces.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern breaks down a whole rabbit to grill with a spicy, Sichuan sauce and plate with panzanella.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern shares his favorite way to prepare wild turkey breast, pan-fried over an open fire. Served with cheese sauce and jalapeno-bacon wild rice. Then, cooks a simple meal of seared salmon and a side of crispy potatoes and onions.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern goes primal with two antelope dishes: a Greek-style whole leg roasted over the open fire with a lemony avgolemono sauce and crispy potatoes, and a simple antelope tenderloin carpaccio served with roasted tomatoes and parmesan.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Zimmern cooks his version of Peking-style goose. Next, Chef Zimmern cooks a meal of striped bass.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern grills his favorite tender cuts of venison and serves it with coal-roasted sweet potatoes. Then, he cooks a hearty pot of classic Louisiana gumbo using wild duck and grills crispy-skinned duck breast for the topping.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern celebrates cherrystone clams with two show-stopping recipes. He prepares classic grilled clams casino with pancetta, then pushes boundaries with an innovative grilled pizza topped with clams, garlic, chiles, and cheese.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern starts simmering a classic Italian Bolognese sauce, with ground bison, lots of red wine and tomatoes. Then, he adds marinated bison skirt steak tacos with borracho beans to the tailgate menu.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern transforms wild duck into a simple grilled appetizer inspired by Japanese tataki and then uses the rest of the duck to make open fire duck noodle soup with fresh egg noodles, lots of aromatic herbs, bean sprouts and squeeze of lime.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern creates an elegant, seared pigeon toast crowned with a Sicilian caponata bursting with grilled eggplant, olives, and capers and a crispy pigeon katsu served with a refreshing shaved cabbage salad and punchy ginger-carrot vinaigrette.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern celebrates summer's bounty with an elegant bass crudo and a whole roasted striped bass topped with an herb-packed compound butter and served alongside a smoky corn succotash studded with charred poblanos.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern elevates California halibut, first with grilled fillets on saffron labneh with romesco and roasted cabbage. Then, he demonstrates a classic salt-crusted whole halibut, served with whole roasted cauliflower and a rich cream sauce.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew butchers a whole pheasant to make yakitori-style skewers and a homemade flatbread, cucumber salad and a mango chutney.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern makes two different dishes with red snapper: fried snapper fingers with a spicy chile-orange mayonnaise dipping sauce and a slow-roasted whole fish with an herb salad and charred miso sweet potatoes.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern reimagines the Hawaiian plate lunch with a hand chopped venison burger topped with homemade gravy and a fried egg, jumbo grilled shrimp, a humble macaroni salad and a heaping scoop of white rice.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern showcases venison two ways: a delicate grilled paillard with artichokes and parmesan, and a rich, slow-braised Polish hunter's stew with kielbasa, mushrooms, and sauerkraut. Served with grilled leeks and coal-roasted potatoes.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah smoke up some State Fair Turkey Legs; fry some skillet Chorizo & Eggs; smoke-and-fry some Buffalo Rib Tips; and cook up some Sriracha and Garlic-Butter Grilled Shrimp.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah are smoking up some BBQ Pulled Pork Sandwiches with Coleslaw; frying up a skillet of Creole Cabbage; and charcoal-grilling some Steak Sandwiches with Chimichurri Mayo.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.desc: Kevin & Noah make their twists on some L.A. outdoor classics: Backyard Burgers 2 Ways; Pit-Smoked Pollo Asado; Grilled Mexican Street Corn and Bacon-Wrapped Danger Dogs.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah are making brisket 3 ways! They're pit-smoking a classic Texas brisket; showing you how to gussy up a pot of Brisket Baked Beans; and making a charcoal-grilled Vietnamese Brisket Noodle Bowl with all the fixings.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah are smoking a humungous Thor's Hammer; cooking up some Catfish & Eggs for breakfast; making a Grilled Salsa Verde; and putting on a pot of Mezcal Charro Beans.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: At the end of a long, hectic day, when you need dinner in a pinch, Frankie unlocks his not-so-secret formula for whipping up quick and easy meals, using minimal ingredients that will satisfy your cravings and feed the family in a flash.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: There may be infinite ways to define pie, but the only math that matters is what makes these sweet and savory dishes fit the bill. In true Struggle Style, Frankie rolls out his best tips and tricks for enjoying hassle free, cost friendly pies.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Brackets are complete and all that's missing is food to fuel your team's run. Huddle around the kitchen as Frankie preps the ultimate watch party spread. These savory, spicy and sweet dishes are championship-worthy even if your bracket goes bust.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: As one of the cheapest members of the produce aisle, bananas will save you bunches of money when you whip up these delightfully appealing recipes that go above and beyond a basic bread.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie takes on his longtime frenemy, the onion, and all its powerful relatives. There might be a few tears shed along the way, but they are well worth the delicious and flavorful results, from sweet buttery leeks to the ultimate onion masterpiece.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Summer is coming to a close but there's still a bounty of fresh, seasonal produce to enjoy. Celebrate with Frankie as he makes some of his favorite cookout classics that'll take you from a weekend picnic to a backyard BBQ, with or without a grill.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie is all about BYOL - bring your own lunch. It's not only good for your wallet, it's good for your health. Skip the vending machine and the cafeteria in favor of creative, cost effective and easy-to-transport midday meals.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Amp up your salad game with a little mixing and matching, from light and leafy greens to crisp and hearty toppings, and dressings for all occasions. Frankie shows us how versatile and fulfilling a homemade salad can truly be.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Apples aren't just good for keeping the doctor away. Frankie demonstrates how to make the most of this beloved, everyday fruit, from sweet treats to savory meals. There's a whole world of apples to dive into, especially while they're in season.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Never underestimate the power of squash: they're affordable, abundant and they come in all shapes, sizes, colors, and textures. Frankie spotlights squash from summer through winter highlighting their amazing superhero traits.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a midweek traybake of roast veg and Camembert. He turns poached chicken into a creamy chicken & chive pie, serves up his easy green chilli salsa under sizzling butterflied prawns, and finishes it all off with decadent chocolate tiramisù.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a speedy jarred-pepper pasta, crispy chicken in baked-bread sauce, and a beef brisket traybake - all prepped in minutes. He makes shelf-raid spiced haricot beans, and rounds it all off with a fast and simple strawberry & balsamic tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie shares a simple salmon dish perfect for a midweek meal, and some no-fuss fish cakes made from tins. He cooks a lamb Madras traybake, as well as a no-cook pasta sauce, and crowns it all off with a hot and crispy ice cream parcel!

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a speedy midweek chicken traybake. He shows us cool ways to upgrade salmon, knocks up a shelf-raid quesadilla toastie, and transforms a batch-cook Bolognese into cheesy bread bowls. All finished off with a super simple banoffee tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a midweek meal of speedy sweet pea chicken, a juicy Smash burger and a chicken and mushroom rice traybake. He batch-cooks his seven-veg sauce, followed by a spicy fish soup. And finishes off with a perfect pudding of tinned fruit granita.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A visit to a strip joint fails to cheer up Chandler; Rachel gets Ross to go to an opera; Charlton Heston finds Joey in a compromising position during a movie shoot.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler runs into Janice, now divorced, and finds her more irritating than ever; Ross plays rugby to impress Emily; Monica tries to figure out what a mysterious electrical switch in her new apartment does.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel throws Emily a phony going-away party so she can see Joshua outside of work, and pregnant Phoebe craves meat.

programme.desc: New Yorker Carlo Cofield (Tony Curtis) goes on a vacation to Southern California, where he quickly becomes immersed in the easy-going local culture while getting entangled in two beachside romances. First, he makes the acquaintance of the lovely Laura (Claudia Cardinale), who seems to like him but is also involved with the laid-back Rod (Robert Webber). Next, Carlo falls for the free-spirited Mali

programme.title: A Fine Pair
programme.desc: A New York police captain (Rock Hudson) helps an Italian beauty (Claudia Cardinale) return some stolen jewels to a safe in Austria.

programme.title: West Point
programme.desc: Wealthy football player Brice Wayne enters West Point and, though he does well on the football field, angers his fellow cadets. He resigns, only to return for the big game and reunite with Betty Channing, the hotel owner's daughter.

programme.title: Secret Service of the Air
programme.desc: Two-fisted shipping pilot Brass Bancroft (Ronald Reagan) is tapped by Secret Service agent Tom Saxby (John Litel) to infiltrate an air freight company that's smuggling illegal aliens over the Mexican border in its planes. Ensnaring lead smuggler Jim Cameron (James Stephenson) in a plot to purchase some counterfeiting plates, Bancroft finds himself endangered when his straight-arrow friend, Gabby W

programme.title: Murder in the Air
programme.desc: Secret Service agents Brass Bancroft (Ronald Reagan) and Gabby Watters (Eddie Foy Jr.) believe they have uncovered an enemy agent: a homeless man found dead in a train crash, who had $50,000 in cash and incriminating tattoos. With this lead, Brass takes the dead man's place to find out what his ultimate goal was. It turns out that the man had ties to terrorists whose intent is to destroy a dirigib

programme.title: Secret Enemies
programme.desc: After the death of a close friend who worked as a secret agent, Carl Becker (Craig Stevens) is spurred to leave his law practice and girlfriend (Faye Emerson) behind to avenge the death of his lost confidante. Becker goes deep undercover to hunt out a dangerous nest of Nazis led by the notorious Dr. Woodford. However, this daring American finds more than he bargained for when he discovers his dupl

programme.title: Born for Trouble
programme.desc: A reporter (Van Johnson) and his colleagues (Faye Emerson, George Meeker) work together to solve the murder of a death-row inmate.

programme.title: The Hidden Hand
programme.desc: The authorities apprehend an escaped mental patient turned serial killer.

programme.title: Private Detective
programme.desc: A female private eye joins forces with a police detective to investigate the suspicious murder of a millionaire.

programme.title: The Last Ride
programme.desc: Brothers on opposite sides of the law clash over a stolen goods ring and their love for the same woman.

programme.title: Truck Busters
programme.desc: A driver leads independent truckers against big-time mob-supported operators who try to run them out of business.

programme.title: Escape From Crime
programme.desc: Red O'Hara (Richard Travis) serves a prison sentence after being framed by Dude Merrill (Paul Fix). Once he's released, he seeks out a job as a news photographer. Although papers are hesitant to hire an ex-convict, he catches a break when he's at the scene of a bank robbery and snaps some pictures. He's hired by Cornell (Frank Wilcox) and begins specializing in crime-scene photos. When he gets win

programme.title: Dark Victory
programme.desc: Socialite Judith Traherne (Bette Davis) lives a lavish but emotionally empty life. Riding horses is one of her few joys, and her stable master (Humphrey Bogart) is secretly in love with her. Told she has a brain tumor by her doctor, Frederick Steele (George Brent), Judith becomes distraught. After she decides to have surgery to remove the tumor, Judith realizes she is in love with Dr. Steele, but

programme.title: 'Til We Meet Again
programme.desc: Time is short for Joan Ames (Merle Oberon). Suffering from a terminal illness, she has only weeks to live and has decided to spend her remaining days traveling the globe. While on a cruise ship back to America, she meets and falls in love with Dan Hardesty (George Brent). Joan doesn't want to ruin their romance by telling him the truth about her illness, but what Joan doesn't know is that Dan's ti

programme.title: Honeymoon for Three
programme.desc: On a book tour stop in Ohio, author Kenneth Bixby (George Brent) is surprised when a young woman, Julie Wilson, attaches herself to him, insisting that they were once college sweethearts. programme.desc: He was so beloved in Pittsburgh that fans created Franco's Italian Army; but you're bound to become popular in the Steel City when you catch The Immaculate Reception, help your team win four Super Bowls, and become a Pro Football Hall of Famer.

programme.title: A Football Life
programme.desc: Following the career and life of LaDainian Tomlinson including his revenge against the Patriots and an emotional trip to learn about his family's roots.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke hops on the train in Ga Thap Cham, and finds himself in Nha Trang- a region of Vietnam with a similar vibe to the French Riviera! Luke hand-catches seafood, looks for some fresh durian fruit, and visits a barramundi farm to harvest sea grapes!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke is in the town of Hoi An. He visits a family who has been making chilli sauce for 5 generations, stops by the Historic Japanese Covered Bridge built in 1719, and meets a 4th generation blacksmith who is creating knives from bomb shells.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: On this stop of his Vietnam journey, Luke visits the bustling seaside city of Da Nang. Join him as he visits the spectacular Dragon Bridge, looks up some succulent shimp on the beach, and stumbles upon a tiny fishing village packed with restaurants.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke visits Huê- famous for its heritage and cuisine and Con Hen Village to learn a technique for de-shelling baby clams, searches for best bowl of spicy beef noodle soup, and stops by a business where they they make turmeric vermicelli noodle salad!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: After a long trip from Hue, Luke finally gets some rest in the scenic Ninh Bihn. Tag along as Luke meets with a local rice farmer, takes a canoe out for some crab hunting, and ventures deep into the jungle in search of a local delicacy- red ant eggs!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke is in northern Vietnam- Hai Phong! This is the birthplace of his partner Lynne. Join Luke as he meets some of Lynne's family for the first time, and tries the delcious Banh Da Noodle- a tasty dish that Hai Phong is renowned for!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke's penultimate stop on his train journey is Hanoi. Follow Luke on his hunt for an iconic Hanoi dish, Bun Cha. Luke takes a ride on the Reunification Express railway, and visits the famous Hoan Kiem Lake for some delcious magenta sticky rice.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Nearing the end of his Vitenamese journey, Luke stops in Sapa, a hilltop region that he remembers from his travels fifteen years ago. Join Luke as he rekindles a friendship from his previous visit and learns about a local dish of dried buffalo.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: In Portugal, in the Douro River Valley, a steam locomotive and a small historic train will take you in turn to the water's edge, on the only railroad line in the world that crosses three World Heritage Sites classified by UNESCO.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: Andalusia, now crossed by high-speed and modernized rail lines, unveils its rich culture and landscapes; along the journey, we visit cities like Seville, Granada with its famed Alhambra and Guadix near the Tabernas desert.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: The Cinque Terre Express runs along Italy's northern coast east of Genoa; passing through five UNESCO-listed villages, it offers coastal views and access to cobbled streets, harbors, terraced vineyards and pastel-colored houses.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie returns to cooking the food he loves the most, getting right to heart of the Italian kitchen.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie gets a masterclass in Sicilian sweets and cooking up an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. There's also some chocolaty cannoli.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie visits Basilicata in search of some old school Italian recipes and meets a family of shepherds and an 89-year-old macaroni master.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie heads for one of the hottest food destinations in Italy, making hand-crafted pasta, a special seafood stew, and the region's answer to paella.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie tracks down some fast, fresh and easy recipes, featuring tasty minty pea tortiglioni and gnocchi with roasted tomato sauce and rocket pesto.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie is taking spaghetti bolognese to the next level and re-creating a mind-blowing Jewish-Italian recipe. There's also beef stew with pappardelle.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie is high up in the Italian Alps cooking some great comfort food, including dumplings, mind-blowing macaroni cheese, and a classic red wine and sausage risotto.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Marseille in the south of France. Inspired by their stunning seafood, the traditional flavors of Provence, and the multicultural dishes being served up there, he cooks up a steak with a twist and a sublime courgette and olive tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie explores Tunisia to meet traditional couscous makers, eat delicious street food, and get invited to cook with local chefs on a beach. Inspired by everything he sees, Jamie cooks crispy prawn parcels and a chicken and merguez sausage stew.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie's soaking up inspiration in Spain with tapas, flavor-charged rice dishes, and spectacular seafood. He cooks a stunning pork chop with peppers and alioli and a surf and turf skewer of prawns, mushrooms, and local ham.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Greece, to the island of Skopelos, starting on the mainland in Thessaloniki. Inspired by meze plates and traditional island cuisine he cooks up a smoky aubergine flatbread and a lemony chargrilled chicken with tzatziki and rice.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke arrives in Saigon for a breakfast of iced coffee with condensed milk and a vermicelli noodle dish. Later, he visits his favourite Pho restaurant, then cooks up some spicy Chilli Crab Claws.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: In Kuala Lumpur, Luke meets the famous Mr Hung with a reputation for flying instant noodles and Mr Sou who cooks a hearty Pork Bone Soup with medicinal herbal properties.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food adventure exploring Jakarta, a melting pot of diverse cultural cuisines, where he indulges in an Indonesian dessert, the sweet Martabak, known as the "King of Street Food".

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke heads to the city of Bangalore in southern India; he visits MTR, one of the oldest coffee houses in the city; he learns how to make idli; he meets up with a friend to explore the night markets.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke learns about Kongunadu cuisine in Coimbatore, partners with a family in Thrissur to create an authentic delicacy and learns about India's rich coffee culture.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke visits the port city of Kochi, Kerala, where he checks out the iconic Chinese fishing nets, does laundry the old-fashioned way and cooks a famous dish. He leaves the city to witness the state's backwaters and local fishing techniques.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke visits the lush plantations of spices in Thekkady and then tours Madurai, the spiritual heart of Tamil Nadu, to find hidden gems with the help of a local food journalist.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke uncovers the history of Chettinad and savours its famous cuisine. He then tastes the chutneys in Kumbakonam and explores the exciting food scene in Pondicherry.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke searches to find Ho Chi Minh's old markets before they disappear due to District 1's rapid development. Join Luke as he gathers fresh ingredients from a local market, tries some traditional delicacies, and follows his sweet tooth to Marou.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke's next stop is the destination of Dalat. Follow Luke as he visits the Art Deco Dalat Train Station, experiences the French influence of the region with a Father and Son chef duo, and discovers why Dalat is known as the'Land Of Eternal Spring!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke hops on the train in Ga Thap Cham, and finds himself in Nha Trang- a region of Vietnam with a similar vibe to the French Riviera! Luke hand-catches seafood, looks for some fresh durian fruit, and visits a barramundi farm to harvest sea grapes!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke is in the town of Hoi An. He visits a family who has been making chilli sauce for 5 generations, stops by the Historic Japanese Covered Bridge built in 1719, and meets a 4th generation blacksmith who is creating knives from bomb shells.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: On this stop of his Vietnam journey, Luke visits the bustling seaside city of Da Nang. Join him as he visits the spectacular Dragon Bridge, looks up some succulent shimp on the beach, and stumbles upon a tiny fishing village packed with restaurants.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke visits Huê- famous for its heritage and cuisine and Con Hen Village to learn a technique for de-shelling baby clams, searches for best bowl of spicy beef noodle soup, and stops by a business where they they make turmeric vermicelli noodle salad!

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati is in the magic town of Maní to learn about something sacred to the Mayans - bees and honey. A group of women who protect the endangered Melipona, a stingless bee native to Yucatán, invites her to join in a Mayan energy cleansing ritual.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: In Tekit, the capital of the Guayabera, Pati encounters a tale of sons going against their father's wishes to follow their own path. Pati meets a farmer's son who went against his father to build a family business making the iconic Guayabera shirts.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati explores the streets of Yucatán's capital, Mérida. She tries the best tacos in Mérida at Wayan'e. The aroma of freshly made cookies lures her to Dondé Fabric, where their globitos and bizcochitos are an important part of Mérida's mornings.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits Chef Wilson Alonzo in his hometown of Halachó to prepare a traditional Cochinita Pibil in an underground pit. Pati helps Wilson prepare the achiote marinade, along with his grandmother who taught him traditional cooking.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati arrives in Motul, a town in the history books for Yucatán's once thriving henequén industry and for the egg dish Huevos Motuleños. Pati meets Doña Evelia, who put Motul back on the map with her world-famous recipe.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Temozón is the birthplace of Yucatán's signature smoked meat Carne Ahumada and everyone in town claims to have a relative who invented it. Pati strolls around town to try different versions of Carne Ahumada.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: Andalusia, now crossed by high-speed and modernized rail lines, unveils its rich culture and landscapes; along the journey, we visit cities like Seville, Granada with its famed Alhambra and Guadix near the Tabernas desert.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: In Greece, the Intercity Express travels from Thessaloniki to Athens, passing sites like the Meteora peaks, Delphi ruins and the Acropolis; the journey continues to the Peloponnese on Greece's last cogwheel train.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: The Little Yellow Train winds through the Eastern Pyrenees, crossing the Cerdan plateau, the historic Gisclard railway suspension bridge and the Grandes Canalettes caves, known as the underground Versailles of the Pyrenees.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: Corsica, the Island of Beauty, is best explored by railway; from Mediterranean waters to towering peaks, stops include Algajola for a seaside dive, Vizzanova on the GR20 trail and historic cities like Corte with its citadel.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: In Portugal, in the Douro River Valley, a steam locomotive and a small historic train will take you in turn to the water's edge, on the only railroad line in the world that crosses three World Heritage Sites classified by UNESCO.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: The Cinque Terre Express runs along Italy's northern coast east of Genoa; passing through five UNESCO-listed villages, it offers coastal views and access to cobbled streets, harbors, terraced vineyards and pastel-colored houses.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: On a mission to taste the worlds best breakfast, the three big egos Gordon, Gino and Fred are venturing out of their comfort zones crossing over the USA boarder into Mexico. They hope to stay out of trouble as they drive their bad boy transporter.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred travel to France, where Fred has been asked to co-host an Oyster Festival in the beautiful Arcachon Bay, in just four days' time. Reunited with their campervan, Fred nominates himself as the driver in his homeland.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: After four weeks on the road Gino's airing his feet in the back seat as the guys cruise along a Texan highway. Texas is a meat lovers paradise and Gordon is keen to show the Europhiles some of the best BBQ known to man.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred start their road trip in Gino's homeland of Italy, where Gino needs help to pull off an event that is close to his heart. Gino collects Gordon and Fred in a campervan to begin a road trip they won't be forgetting in a hurry.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes Fred to the New Forest, where fresh local produce is king. Featuring wood-fired cooking, pork pies and a country pub serving the perfect Ploughman's.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix is heading to Bristol for a winter treat in the company of Michel Roux Junior. Michel can't wait to show Fred his favourites, from the perfect French bistro to a festive countryside escape and Jamaican jerk turkey.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Chef Nadiya Hussain shows Fred the culinary sights of Yorkshire including Bettys' iconic tea and cakes, a rural Wensleydale cheese farm and shop, and a small restaurant which serves home made samosas, Nadiya's favourite spicy Indian snack.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Food writer Rachel Khoo takes Fred to Vienna to experience exquisite coffee and cake, comforting dumplings, and mangalitza pork schnitzel.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Restaurateur Thomasina Miers takes Fred to South Wales to explore some wild places to eat from an ancient inn to a beachside restaurant and wood fired cooking.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Pastry Chef Ravneet Gill takes Fred to Mallorca to experience incredible island cooking, from a buzzing beach bar serving holiday food in the sun, to light and fluffy pastries.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie visits the pizza capital of Italy to learn recipes from the locals and tries acquapazza, fish poached in crazy water.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie gets a masterclass in Sicilian sweets and cooking up an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. There's also some chocolaty cannoli.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie returns to cooking the food he loves the most, getting right to heart of the Italian kitchen.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie tracks down some fast, fresh and easy recipes, featuring tasty minty pea tortiglioni and gnocchi with roasted tomato sauce and rocket pesto.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie is high up in the Italian Alps cooking some great comfort food, including dumplings, mind-blowing macaroni cheese, and a classic red wine and sausage risotto.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie heads for one of the hottest food destinations in Italy, making hand-crafted pasta, a special seafood stew, and the region's answer to paella.

programme.title: Cleopatra
programme.desc: The queen of Egypt (Elizabeth Taylor) seduces Julius Caesar (Rex Harrison), but when he is killed, she uses Mark Antony (Richard Burton) as her new protector.

programme.title: The Last Time I Saw Paris
programme.desc: Sudden money ruins a struggling writer (Van Johnson) and his wife (Elizabeth Taylor) in post-World War II Paris.

programme.title: Berlin Express
programme.desc: International train passengers (Merle Oberon, Robert Ryan, Charles Korvin) comb postwar Frankfurt for a politician abducted by Nazis.

programme.title: House of Bamboo
programme.desc: An Army agent (Robert Stack) infiltrates a sadistic ex-GI's (Robert Ryan) gang in postwar Tokyo.

programme.title: The Hustler
programme.desc: A gambler (George C. Scott) stakes pool shark Fast Eddie Felson (Paul Newman) in a smoke-filled marathon against Minnesota Fats (Jackie Gleason).

programme.title: The Laughing Policeman
programme.desc: Two San Francisco detectives (Walter Matthau, Bruce Dern) clash while trying to solve a mass murder of bus passengers.

programme.title: Fractured Flickers
programme.desc: Hans Conried and a celebrity guest present a succession of film clips from the silent picture era dubbed over with comedic dialogue and sound effects.

programme.title: Impact
programme.desc: A declared-dead man (Brian Donlevy) hides out with a widow (Ella Raines) after his wife and her lover botch his murder.

programme.title: Harper
programme.desc: A rich woman (Lauren Bacall) hires gum-chewing Los Angeles private eye Lew Harper (Paul Newman) to look for her missing husband.

programme.title: The Laughing Policeman
programme.desc: Two San Francisco detectives (Walter Matthau, Bruce Dern) clash while trying to solve a mass murder of bus passengers.

programme.title: The Park Is Mine
programme.desc: An armed Vietnam veteran (Tommy Lee Jones) takes over New York's Central Park to publicize the plight of veterans.

programme.title: The Last American Hero
programme.desc: A North Carolina moonshiner becomes a professional stock-car racer to raise money to free his jailed father.

programme.title: Breaking Away
programme.desc: An Indiana teen (Dennis Christopher) acts Italian as he and his townie buddies (Dennis Quaid, Daniel Stern) compete with college snobs.

programme.title: Panic in Needle Park
programme.desc: A woman from Indiana becomes addicted to heroin when she falls in love with a larcenous New York City junkie.

programme.title: The Rose
programme.desc: An exhausted rock star (Bette Midler) turns to drugs, alcohol and a lover (Frederic Forrest) when her greedy manager (Alan Bates) will not let her quit.

programme.title: A Star Is Born
programme.desc: A matinee idol turns to alcohol when his wife's acting career starts to take off and his own popularity begins to dwindle.

programme.title: Borderline
programme.desc: A U.S. Border Patrol officer (Charles Bronson) poses as an illegal alien to catch a killer (Ed Harris) smuggling laborers from Mexico.

programme.title: Happy Land
programme.desc: An Iowa drugstore owner (Don Ameche) mourns his soldier son; his own father's ghost (Harry Carey) gives him hope.

programme.title: Detour
programme.desc: A down-and-out piano player becomes involved with a mysterious woman and two murders as he hitchhikes west.

programme.title: Cowboy
programme.desc: A Chicago hotel desk clerk (Jack Lemmon) talks a trail boss (Glenn Ford) into taking him on a cattle drive to Mexico.

programme.title: Glengarry Glen Ross
programme.desc: Underhanded real estate salesmen sink to varying levels of deceit in order to sell their dubious properties.

programme.title: The Paper Chase
programme.desc: A first-year law student (Timothy Bottoms) dates his toughest professor's (John Houseman) daughter (Lindsay Wagner).

programme.title: The Driver
programme.desc: An obsessed police detective (Bruce Dern) chases a professional driver (Ryan O'Neal) of getaway cars.

programme.title: The French Connection
programme.desc: New York Detective Popeye Doyle (Gene Hackman) and his partner (Roy Scheider) chase a French heroin smuggler.

programme.title: Zorba the Greek
programme.desc: A lusty Greek peasant (Anthony Quinn) shows a British writer (Alan Bates) how to live and run a lignite mine.

programme.title: The Flim Flam Man
programme.desc: An Army deserter (Michael Sarrazin) joins an aging Southerner (George C. Scott) working scams from town to town.

programme.title: Hopscotch
programme.desc: An ex-CIA agent (Walter Matthau) and his lover (Glenda Jackson) write a book about the agency, one jump ahead of agents sent to stop them.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob brings herds of jellyfish to Mr. Krabs; Patrick gets a job at the Chum Bucket and competes with SpongeBob in the Fry Cook Games.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Squidward and SpongeBob plan a talent show; SpongeBob thinks Plankton is lonely.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton tries to befriend Squidward so he can get the Krabby Patty formula; the citizens of Bikini Bottom clamor for Patrick's artwork after an art critic praises him.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Patrick has a coupon for free ice cream, but he has to get to the store if he wants to use it; Mr. Krabs buys Squidward's art, but to make a profit on his purchase he will have to get the artist out of the picture.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob befriends a sentient Krabby Patty whose only wish is to be eaten; Rube hosts an episode of "FUN-Believable! With Rube" where he explores little known oddities and attractions around Bikini Bottom.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob hides from Sandy; SpongeBob and Patrick borrow a balloon from a store and accidentally pop it.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Sandy creates the latest acorn-based food sensation, but her tree pays the price for her greed; SpongeBob puts up a community bulletin board at the Krusty Krab, but anonymous reviews lead to trouble.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob fails his driving test again; SpongeBob and Patrick go to a jellyfishing convention.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton poses as a wish-granting genie in his latest scheme to get the secret formula; customers love Mr. Krabs' new krabby soup, until some heat-loving hippies move into the soup vat.
